name: Moonlighter
short_description: Moonlighter is an Action RPG with rogue-lite elements that demonstrates two sides of the coin – revealing everyday routines of Will, an adventurous shopkeeper that secretly dreams of becoming a hero.
detailed_description:
Since launch, Moonlighter has received a wealth of updates—from quality-of-life improvements and balance tweaks to major roadmap additions. The current version expands the original experience with new mini-bosses, gear, over 100 new room layouts, Companions, deeper lore, and a New Game+ mode packed with fresh challenges.

But Will is an unwavering adventurer, and his story doesn’t end here. In 2026, Moonlighter 2: The Endless Vault—now available in Early Access—will receive its full release, expanding on every aspect of the beloved shopkeeping action-RPG formula and reimagining it with vibrant, fully 3D visuals.

During a long-passed archaeological excavation, a set of Gates were discovered. People quickly realized that these ancient passages lead to different realms and dimensions – providing brave and reckless adventurers with treasures beyond measure. Rynoka, a small commercial village, was founded near the excavation site providing refuge and a place for adventurers to sell their hard-earned riches.

Moonlighter is an Action RPG with rogue-lite elements following the everyday routines of Will, an adventurous shopkeeper that dreams of becoming a hero.

SHOPKEEPING

While conducting your business in Rynoka village, you can put items on sale, set their price carefully, manage gold reserves, recruit assistants and upgrade the shop. Be careful though – some shady individuals may want to steal your precious wares!

FIGHT WITH STYLE

Defeat various enemies and bosses, and revel in challenging and deep combat mechanics. Masterful control of your weapons, critical timing, careful positioning, and an understanding of your enemies and environment are crucial to your survival. How you battle your enemies is up to you.

MEET VILLAGERS

Get to know your neighbors as you restore the prosperity of this small commercial hamlet. Help establish new businesses and watch them grow in the idyllic community of Rynoka.

CRAFT AND ENCHANT

Understanding the crafting and enchanting system is essential for your progression. Interact with the villagers to craft new armor and weapons, and enchant existing equipment. This creates a great deal of flexibility and spices up the way equipment is used.

GET THE LOOT

Access strange worlds through otherworldly gates and collect valuable items from exotic civilizations: resources, weapons, armors and peculiar artifacts. Hoarding everything won't be possible - use the unique inventory system wisely to take the most profitable loot with you.

BEFRIEND COMPANIONS

Gates lead to different worlds. Each run is unique and forces you to make smart and calculated decisions. You never know what you will encounter next – that’s why you must be prepared for everything.

supported_languages: English, French, German, Spanish - Spain, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Polish, Russian, Korean, Portuguese - Brazil, Italian, Turkish, Traditional Chinese

reviews.review_text: i originally played this game in middle school 8 years ago. and no game has been more influential in the way i think about video games. while it may not be for everyone i remember when I first got into the flow of looting and learning how to manage a tricky inventory full of cursed items. all of this was 7 years ago and even as my tastes have grown as i have. my heart will always be in rynoka. excited to try ML2
Clunky movement and enemies that aren't on par with the amazing pixel art.

This game is very good, just PLEASE USE A CONTROLLER.
It’s a dungeon kind of game but you sell the stuff you get. It’s a pretty unique concept and is worth playing, especially since it goes on great sale. I would recommend it just dont play on pc the controls are TERRIBLE.

Great vibes and music. A good indie game.

Enjoyed the shopkeeping aspect, and the happy sounds of the customers remained satisfying until the end. Estimating the prices of items was fun enough and the balanced game flow of dungeon crawling and selling the loot was a good fit for the length of the game.

I really wish the quality of life for demand of items and their prices was as good as selling items in neutral demand (price is automatically noted down, but if high demand, then nothing is noted down, and you have to remember it yourself). I kinda didn't like how time still passed while in menus during the shop's opening hours, but it did insentivize strategic setup of the initial items put on display.

Quest system was kinda bothersome and npcs in town could have used a purpose. Some side stories with quest events in the dungeons would have been cool. It seems fleshing out the core gameplay loop, polishing the other minor features and including some quality of life features like the note was prioritized instead, and I think that was absolutely the right call.

A bit rigid combat. I wished it was less about corner clipping enemies with attacks, and more about timing and combos. I also found the attack range of weapons finicky. Once I found out about charge attacks however, and that many enemies actually doesn't hurt on contact, the combat really opened up and I found the game a lot more engaging. I especially like the fists with their combo attacks and charge dash attack.

I wish I could queue up a weapon swap while finishing an attack. Feels wonky that I have to be idle to swap weapons. I also wish I could see on my character, which weapon is active, instead of looking at the top right corner of the UI. So many times I've tried to do a fancy weapon swap combo, only to unexpectedly use the wrong weapon.

Some way to auto sell low quality loot would have been nice, since I don't like having to play menus to min/max.
I also felt the demon to kick you out if you're too slow was a weird fit for the game, but it wasn't too much of a bother.

Despite my gripes about the combat, the varied enemy design really pulled through and I had a good time running around punching stuff.

Controls are atrocious, analog stick movement allows for immediate dodge rolls but stops you from attacking, d-pad movement allows you to immediately attack in place but will not allow you to dodge without stopping. Keyboard and mouse controls are laughably bad as well. Don't play this if you value gameplay.
